title: Calonemorchis stellata
taxonomy: Calonemorchis stellata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which includes approximately 32,000 species of orchids, making it one of the largest and most diverse plant families. This classification places Calonemorchis stellata within the order Asparagales, a group of monocots that also includes agaves, asparagus, and hyacinths. The genus Calonemorchis is a small group of orchids, with Calonemorchis stellata being one of the few species within this genus. The family Orchidaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including the subfamily Epidendroideae, to which Calonemorchis stellata belongs. Further classification of Calonemorchis stellata within the subfamily Epidendroideae is ongoing and subject to change.
uses: Calonemorchis stellata has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its leaves and stems are sometimes employed in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and to confirm its safety for human use. Some studies suggest that the plant may exhibit antioxidant and antiviral activities, which could be beneficial in the prevention and treatment of certain diseases. Nevertheless, the use of Calonemorchis stellata for medicinal purposes should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.

intro: Calonemorchis stellata, also known as the star orchid, is a species of flowering orchid native to New Guinea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of infections, injuries, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Calonemorchis stellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Calonemorchis stellata is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
